title: anisomeles farinacea
intro: Anisomeles farinacea, also known as Indian hyptis or khada, is a perennial herb native to tropical Asia, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Anisomeles farinacea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and treat digestive problems like diarrhea and nausea. Anisomeles farinacea can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being applied topically as an ointment or cream to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
